name: Workshop Computer
description: Programmable 8HP music computer with hot-swappable program cards. Three knobs and a mode switch control cards that turn the module into sequencers, effects, MIDI bridges, or custom code.

controls.name: Knob X
controls.description: First auxiliary parameter for the active program card.

controls.name: Knob Y
controls.description: Second auxiliary parameter for the active program card.

controls.name: Mode switch
controls.description: Three-way switch used by many cards to select operating state or alternate behavior.

controls.name: Program card slot
controls.description: Hot-swappable firmware slot. The inserted card defines the module's current personality.

controls.name: Card load / reset button
controls.description: Reboots or loads the inserted card after power cycling or a manual reset.

inputs.name: Audio/CV In 1
inputs.description: Card-configurable lane for audio or control voltage.

inputs.name: Audio/CV In 2
inputs.description: Second card-configurable lane for audio or control voltage.

inputs.name: Precision CV In 1
inputs.description: Precision control-voltage lane, typically used for pitch or exact parameter tracking.

inputs.name: Pulse In 2
inputs.description: Second trigger, gate, or clock lane.

outputs.name: Audio/CV Out 1
outputs.description: Card-configurable lane for audio or control voltage.

outputs.name: Audio/CV Out 2
outputs.description: Second card-configurable lane for audio or control voltage.

outputs.name: Precision CV Out 1
outputs.description: Precision control-voltage lane, typically used for pitch or exact parameter tracking.

outputs.name: Pulse Out 1
outputs.description: Card-configurable trigger, gate, or clock output.

outputs.name: Pulse Out 2
outputs.description: Second trigger, gate, or clock output.

behaviors.name: Hot-swappable cards
behaviors.description: The active firmware changes immediately when a different card is loaded, so the same 8HP panel can behave like a sequencer, reverb, utility rack, or synth voice.

behaviors.name: Reverb+ card
behaviors.description: The card exposes a CV-controllable hall reverb plus random pulse and CV utilities, including freeze and Turing-Machine-style behavior.

behaviors.name: Turing Machine card
behaviors.description: The module becomes a random looping sequencer with tap-tempo clocking, multiple scales, pulse outputs, and CV lanes that can lock or drift.

behaviors.name: Simple MIDI card
behaviors.description: The computer acts as a USB MIDI bridge that moves note, gate, and controller data between the modular and a host device.

behaviors.name: Blank-card programming
behaviors.description: Blank cards store user code, so the module can run custom RP2040 programs instead of only the shipped card set.

behaviors.name: Card memory as firmware
behaviors.description: The card contains the program image rather than the panel hardware itself, which makes the module effectively reconfigurable software in Eurorack form.

patchIdeas.name: Turing Machine bassline
patchIdeas.patch: Load the Turing Machine card · tap a clock or feed one in · use the CV lane for pitch and the pulse lane for drum accents · one module becomes a repeatable random sequencer.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Workshop Computer:
* Card: Turing Machine
* Mode switch: default

Clock:
Quantizer:
VCO:
Envelope:
VCA:
Kick:
Output:

- Clock (Out) c> Workshop Computer (Pulse In 2)
- Workshop Computer (Pulse Out 1) t> Kick (Trig)
- Workshop Computer (Precision CV Out 2) p> Quantizer (In)
- Quantizer (Out) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- Workshop Computer (Pulse Out 2) t> Envelope (Trig)
- VCO (Out) -> VCA (In)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCA (CV)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Reverb freeze wash
patchIdeas.patch: Load Reverb+ · feed stereo audio into the top row · freeze with a pulse into the freeze input · modulate decay and wet mix from slow CV for evolving frozen pads.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Workshop Computer:
* Card: Reverb+
* Main knob: wet/dry
* Knob X: decay
* Knob Y: tone

Pad:
LFO:
Clock:
Mixer:

- Pad (Out L) -> Workshop Computer (Audio/CV In 1)
- Pad (Out R) -> Workshop Computer (Audio/CV In 2)
- LFO (Out) >> Workshop Computer (Precision CV In 1)
- LFO (Out) >> Workshop Computer (Precision CV In 2)
- Clock (Out) g> Workshop Computer (Pulse In 1) // freeze
- Workshop Computer (Audio/CV Out 1) -> Mixer (In L)
- Workshop Computer (Audio/CV Out 2) -> Mixer (In R)

patchIdeas.name: USB-to-modular bridge
patchIdeas.patch: Load Simple MIDI · send note, gate, and controller data from a computer into the modular · use the precision CV lanes for pitch and the pulse lanes for envelopes and drum hits.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Workshop Computer:
* Card: Simple MIDI

MIDI Controller:
Quantizer:
VCO:
Envelope:
Drum:
Output:

- MIDI Controller (Note) -> Workshop Computer (Audio/CV In 1)
- MIDI Controller (Gate) -> Workshop Computer (Pulse In 1)
- Workshop Computer (Precision CV Out 1) p> Quantizer (In)
- Quantizer (Out) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- Workshop Computer (Pulse Out 1) t> Envelope (Trig)
- Workshop Computer (Pulse Out 2) t> Drum (Trig)
- VCO (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Toolbox utility card
patchIdeas.patch: Load a utility program card from the library · use the computer as a small source bank for clock, noise, and sample-and-hold duties · fold it into a bigger modular patch.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Workshop Computer:
* Card: Toolbox

Filter:
VCA:
Clock:
Noise:
Envelope:
Output:

- Workshop Computer (Pulse Out 1) c> Clock (In)
- Workshop Computer (Audio/CV Out 1) -> Noise (In)
- Workshop Computer (Audio/CV Out 2) -> VCA (In)
- Workshop Computer (Precision CV Out 1) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- Workshop Computer (Pulse Out 2) t> Envelope (Trig)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCA (CV)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Bytebeat percussion
patchIdeas.patch: Load a bytebeat-style card · run it at audio or clock rate · treat the computer as a compact digital percussion source with built-in modulation and pulse outs.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Workshop Computer:
* Card: Bytebeat

Clock:
Filter:
VCA:
Envelope:
Output:

- Clock (Out) c> Workshop Computer (Pulse In 2)
- Workshop Computer (Audio/CV Out 1) -> Filter (In)
- Workshop Computer (Precision CV Out 1) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- Workshop Computer (Pulse Out 1) t> Envelope (Trig)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCA (CV)
- Filter (Out) -> VCA (In)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)
